description: Fix CancellationException being swallowed by generic catch block, and dispose invokeOnCompletion handle after download completes to prevent holding reference to call.
steps: Add catch (e: CancellationException) block BEFORE the generic catch (e: Exception) block that deletes tmpFile and rethrows
Store DisposableHandle from invokeOnCompletion and dispose it in a finally block after the download logic
acceptance_criteria: CancellationException is caught before generic Exception, tmpFile is deleted, and the exception is rethrown
invokeOnCompletion handle is disposed after download completes (success or failure)
verification: Run ./gradlew assembleDebug (or verify Kotlin syntax correctness if SDK unavailable)
findings: Gradle build cannot run due to network timeout downloading Gradle distribution (same as FEAT-001/FEAT-002). Code verified via manual review - uses standard Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines patterns (CancellationException, DisposableHandle.dispose()). The nested try/finally correctly scopes handle disposal while the outer try/catch handles CancellationException before the generic Exception catch.
